    - "Selecting a team in the dropdown renders the Inventory tab as a table with columns Name, Kind, Status (colored badge), Environment, Project — populated from `GET /resources?team=<team>` (D-08, UI-01)"
    - "The Deployments tab shows the same table plus an Environment filter dropdown (All / dev / staging / prod) that filters client-side without a new network request (D-08, UI-02)"
    - "Clicking Refresh re-fetches data via TanStack Query `invalidateQueries`; no automatic polling occurs; `staleTime: Infinity` and `refetchOnWindowFocus: false` are configured (D-09)"
    - "Status badge colors match CLI STATUS_COLORS exactly: pending=amber, provisioning=blue, ready=green, failed=red (UI-SPEC color contract)"

---

<objective>
Deliver the read-only React dashboard at `ui/` (UI-01, UI-02). Single-page app with two tabs (Inventory / Deployments), team dropdown in top nav, manual Refresh button, and a status badge palette that matches the CLI exactly. Scaffolded from scratch with Vite + React 19 + Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui + TanStack Query v5, consuming the `GET /teams` and `GET /resources` endpoints stabilized by Plan 01 (with CORS pre-configured).

Purpose: This is the demo-visible deliverable for stakeholders — the dashboard side of "watch the CLI provision, then open the dashboard to see the team inventory." MVP vertical slice = scaffold + thin end-to-end render of the Inventory tab from real API data, then layer in Deployments + manual refresh.
Output: A `ui/` directory that runs via `cd ui && npm run dev` on port 5173, fetches data through the Vite proxy, and renders the inventory and deployments views per UI-SPEC.

This plan is **NOT autonomous** — it ends with a `checkpoint:human-verify` because UI visual correctness cannot be asserted by automated tests at the pixel/interaction level for a greenfield SPA.
</objective>

<tasks>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Scaffold `ui/` — Vite + React 19 + Tailwind v4 + shadcn init + TanStack Query + component generation; configure Vite proxy</name>
  <files>ui/package.json, ui/vite.config.ts, ui/components.json, ui/tsconfig.json, ui/tsconfig.app.json, ui/tsconfig.node.json, ui/index.html, ui/src/index.css, ui/src/main.tsx, ui/src/lib/queryClient.ts, ui/src/components/ui/*</files>
  <read_first>
    - .planning/phases/05-account-flow-ui-demo-readiness/05-RESEARCH.md — sections: "Standard Stack (JavaScript — UI)", "Installation (UI scaffold sequence)", "Package Legitimacy Audit", "Vite proxy configuration" code example, Pitfall 2 (proxy rewrite), Pitfall 4 (Tailwind v4 has no config file).
    - .planning/phases/05-account-flow-ui-demo-readiness/05-UI-SPEC.md — sections: "Design System", "Component Inventory" (table, badge, tabs, select, button, separator, skeleton), "shadcn Gate Result" (executor must run `npm create vite` then `npx shadcn init`).
    - .planning/phases/05-account-flow-ui-demo-readiness/05-PATTERNS.md — "Vite proxy configuration", "ui/src/lib/queryClient.ts" section.
  </read_first>
  <action>
    Run from repo root in sequence (the scaffold itself is non-revertible without `rm -rf ui/`):

    1. `npm create vite@latest ui -- --template react-ts` — confirm via the prompts that `react-ts` template is selected. Working directory remains repo root after this command; subsequent commands `cd ui` first.

    2. `cd ui && npm install` to install scaffold defaults.

    3. `cd ui && npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite @types/node` — Tailwind v4 + Vite plugin (per RESEARCH Pitfall 4: no `tailwind.config.ts` is needed).

    4. `cd ui && npm install @tanstack/react-query` — TanStack Query v5 (object-form API only — Pitfall 3).

    5. `cd ui && npm install lucide-react` — for `RefreshCw`, `PackageOpen`, `AlertCircle` icons.

    6. Edit `ui/vite.config.ts` to add the `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin and the `/api` proxy (verbatim from RESEARCH Code Examples — Pitfall 2 mitigated by the `rewrite` function): `{ plugins: [react(), tailwindcss()], resolve: { alias: { '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src') } }, server: { proxy: { '/api': { target: 'http://localhost:8000', changeOrigin: true, rewrite: (path) => path.replace(/^\/api/, '') } } } }`. Add `import path from 'path'` and `import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'`.

    7. Edit `ui/src/index.css` to include `@import "tailwindcss";` as the first line (Tailwind v4 CSS-first approach — Pitfall 4). Keep any shadcn-required CSS variable scaffolding that `shadcn init` will inject in step 8.

    8. Edit `ui/tsconfig.json` to add the path alias matching the Vite alias: `compilerOptions: { ..., baseUrl: ".", paths: { "@/*": ["./src/*"] } }`. shadcn init requires this alias to be configured.

    9. `cd ui && npx shadcn@latest init` — answer prompts per UI-SPEC: base color = slate (the default), CSS variables = yes, Tailwind CSS = yes, style = new-york (per RESEARCH State of the Art). Executor should not auto-confirm without reading — if prompts diverge, document in the SUMMARY.

    10. `cd ui && npx shadcn@latest add table badge tabs select button separator skeleton` — installs the 7 components needed per UI-SPEC Component Inventory.

    11. Create `ui/src/lib/queryClient.ts` per UI-SPEC and PATTERNS.md — `QueryClient` with `defaultOptions: { queries: { staleTime: Infinity, refetchOnWindowFocus: false } }`.

    12. Edit `ui/src/main.tsx` to import `QueryClientProvider` from `@tanstack/react-query`, import `queryClient` from `./lib/queryClient`, and wrap `<App />` in `<QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>...</QueryClientProvider>` inside `<React.StrictMode>`.

    13. Edit `ui/index.html` to set the page title to `PECP` and html lang to `en`.

    14. Do NOT delete the Vite default `App.tsx` placeholder in this task — Task 2 replaces it. This task ends with `npm run dev` starting successfully and rendering the Vite default page (or a minimal scaffolded page) at localhost:5173 — the goal is verified scaffold + Tailwind + shadcn-ready ground.

    Verify Pitfall 4 holds: do NOT create `tailwind.config.ts` manually. If `shadcn init` did not create one, leave it absent. Verify Pitfall 2 holds: `vite.config.ts` contains the `rewrite` function.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd /Users/punitlad/projects/work/pecp-poc/ui &amp;&amp; npx tsc --noEmit &amp;&amp; npm run build</automated>
  </verify>
  <acceptance_criteria>
    - Directory `ui/` exists with `package.json`, `vite.config.ts`, `index.html`, `tsconfig.json`, `src/` subtree
    - `grep -nE "\"react\"" ui/package.json` returns a match with version starting with `19` or `^19`
    - `grep -nE "\"@tanstack/react-query\"" ui/package.json` returns a match with version starting with `5` or `^5`
    - `grep -nE "\"tailwindcss\"" ui/package.json` returns a match with version starting with `4` or `^4`
    - `grep -nE "\"@tailwindcss/vite\"" ui/package.json` returns a match
    - `grep -nE "\"vite\"" ui/package.json` returns a match
    - `grep -nE "\"lucide-react\"" ui/package.json` returns a match
    - File `ui/components.json` exists
    - `grep -nE "rewrite:.*replace.*\\^/api" ui/vite.config.ts` returns 1 match (Pitfall 2)
    - `grep -nE "target: 'http://localhost:8000'" ui/vite.config.ts | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns 1 match
    - `grep -nE "tailwindcss\\(\\)" ui/vite.config.ts | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns 1 match (Tailwind plugin registered)
    - `grep -nE "staleTime: Infinity" ui/src/lib/queryClient.ts | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns 1 match
    - `grep -nE "refetchOnWindowFocus: false" ui/src/lib/queryClient.ts | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns 1 match
    - `grep -nE "QueryClientProvider" ui/src/main.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns at least 1 match
    - `grep -nE "@import \"tailwindcss\"" ui/src/index.css` returns 1 match (Pitfall 4)
    - `test ! -f ui/tailwind.config.ts` succeeds (or its existence is documented as shadcn-generated; Pitfall 4 prefers absence)
    - Each shadcn component file exists: `ui/src/components/ui/table.tsx`, `badge.tsx`, `tabs.tsx`, `select.tsx`, `button.tsx`, `separator.tsx`, `skeleton.tsx`
    - `cd ui && npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0
    - `cd ui && npm run build` exits 0 (production build succeeds)
  </acceptance_criteria>
  <done>
    `ui/` is a working Vite + React 19 + Tailwind v4 + shadcn-initialized + TanStack-Query-ready scaffold. The 7 shadcn components are installed under `ui/src/components/ui/`. The Vite proxy is configured to forward `/api/*` to `localhost:8000` with the prefix stripped. `npm run dev` starts the server on 5173 without errors.
  </done>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 2: Implement App.tsx + TopNav + StatusBadge + InventoryTable + DeploymentsTable + hooks — render the full UI-SPEC contract</name>
  <files>ui/src/App.tsx, ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/hooks/useTeams.ts, ui/src/hooks/useResources.ts, ui/src/components/TopNav.tsx, ui/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx, ui/src/components/InventoryTable.tsx, ui/src/components/DeploymentsTable.tsx</files>
  <read_first>
    - .planning/phases/05-account-flow-ui-demo-readiness/05-UI-SPEC.md — entire file (this is the implementation contract for layout, colors, copy, states, interaction). Pay special attention to: "Layout Structure", "Status Badge Color Palette", "Interaction Contract", "States" (loading/empty/error/no-team), "Copywriting Contract".
    - .planning/phases/05-account-flow-ui-demo-readiness/05-PATTERNS.md — every UI Files section (queryClient, api.ts, hooks, StatusBadge, InventoryTable, DeploymentsTable, TopNav, App.tsx). These contain the exact TypeScript shape to mirror.
    - .planning/phases/05-account-flow-ui-demo-readiness/05-RESEARCH.md — TanStack Query Pattern 3 (Pitfall 3: v5 object form only), Code Examples (StatusBadge, useResources, vite.config).
    - .planning/phases/05-account-flow-ui-demo-readiness/05-CONTEXT.md — D-07 (no React Router; local useState for tab), D-08 (column order), D-09 (manual refresh — staleTime Infinity, refetch via invalidateQueries).
    - src/pecp/cli/main.py — locate the STATUS_COLORS mapping (~lines 37-42) to confirm CLI badge color names; UI Tailwind palette must convey the same semantic mapping.
    - src/pecp/api/routes/resources.py — confirm the JSON shape of `GET /resources?team=<team>` (field names: `id, team, kind, name, status, env, project, created_at, ...`). The `Resource` TypeScript type must use these field names verbatim.
    - src/pecp/api/routes/teams.py — confirm `GET /teams` response shape from Plan 01: `[{id, name}]`. The `Team` TypeScript type uses these.
  </read_first>
  <action>
    Implement the following files. Do NOT inline implementations in this action — full patterns are in 05-PATTERNS.md "UI Files" sections.

    1. **`ui/src/lib/api.ts`**:
       - Export TypeScript types: `interface Team { id: string; name: string }`. `interface Resource { id: string; team: string; kind: string; name: string; status: string; env: string | null; project: string | null; created_at?: string }`.
       - `export async function fetchTeams(): Promise<Team[]>` — `await fetch('/api/teams?limit=50')` ... throw on non-OK, return JSON.
       - `export async function fetchResources(team: string): Promise<Resource[]>` — `await fetch('/api/resources?team=' + encodeURIComponent(team))` ... throw on non-OK, return JSON.

    2. **`ui/src/hooks/useTeams.ts`**:
       - `import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'`, `import { fetchTeams, Team } from '../lib/api'`.
       - Export `function useTeams() { return useQuery<Team[]>({ queryKey: ['teams'], queryFn: fetchTeams }); }`.
       - v5 object form only (Pitfall 3).

    3. **`ui/src/hooks/useResources.ts`**:
       - `useResources(team: string | null)` returning `useQuery<Resource[]>({ queryKey: ['resources', team], queryFn: () => fetchResources(team!), enabled: !!team })`.

    4. **`ui/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx`**:
       - Color palette EXACTLY per UI-SPEC Status Badge table: `pending → bg-amber-100 text-amber-700`, `provisioning → bg-blue-100 text-blue-700`, `ready → bg-green-100 text-green-700`, `failed → bg-red-100 text-red-700`. Unknown → `bg-slate-100 text-slate-600`.
       - Render an inline `<span className={`rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 text-xs font-medium ${cls}`}>{status}</span>`. Do NOT use `<Badge variant=...>` (UI-SPEC explicitly forbids it — defaults do not match).
       - Export both the component and the `STATUS_CLASSES` map so tests/dev tools can introspect.

    5. **`ui/src/components/InventoryTable.tsx`**:
       - Props: `{ resources: Resource[]; isLoading: boolean; isError: boolean }`.
       - Use shadcn `<Table>` from `@/components/ui/table`. Header columns in order: Name, Kind, Status, Environment, Project (D-08).
       - Column header style: `text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-wide` (UI-SPEC Typography + Inventory Table).
       - Loading: render 3 `<TableRow>` with `<Skeleton className="h-4 w-full" />` in each `<TableCell>` per UI-SPEC States > Loading.
       - Empty (not loading, no error, `resources.length === 0`): centered `<PackageOpen />` icon (48px, `text-muted-foreground`), heading "No resources found", body "Select a different team or run the seed script to populate data." Match UI-SPEC copywriting verbatim.
       - Error: centered `<AlertCircle />` icon (48px, `text-destructive`), heading "Failed to load resources", body "Check that the PECP API is running on port 8000, then click Refresh."
       - Row: name in first cell, kind plain text, `<StatusBadge status={r.status} />` in status cell, `r.env ?? '—'`, `r.project ?? '—'`.
       - Row hover: `hover:bg-muted/50`. Row min-height 44px (UI-SPEC Spacing exceptions).

    6. **`ui/src/components/DeploymentsTable.tsx`**:
       - Props: `{ resources: Resource[]; isLoading: boolean; isError: boolean }`.
       - Local state `const [envFilter, setEnvFilter] = useState<string>('All')`.
       - Above the table: shadcn `<Select>` with options "All", "dev", "staging", "prod" — label "Environment" (UI-SPEC Copywriting Contract).
       - Filter: `const filtered = envFilter === 'All' ? resources : resources.filter(r => r.env === envFilter)`.
       - Pass `filtered` into the same table structure as InventoryTable (extract a shared `<ResourceTable>` if convenient).
       - All non-filter behavior identical to InventoryTable (loading/empty/error states).

    7. **`ui/src/components/TopNav.tsx`**:
       - Props: `{ selectedTeam: string | null; onTeamChange: (team: string) => void; activeTabQueryKey: unknown[] }`.
       - Layout: `h-14`, `border-b border-border`, `bg-card`, horizontal flex with logo on left ("PECP" in `text-xl font-semibold`), team dropdown center-left, refresh button on right.
       - Team dropdown: shadcn `<Select>` populated from `useTeams()`. Disabled while teams loading. Placeholder "Select a team". Loading text "Loading teams...". On change, call `onTeamChange`.
       - Refresh button: shadcn `<Button>` with `<RefreshCw className={`h-4 w-4 mr-2 ${isFetching ? 'animate-spin' : ''}`} />Refresh`. `onClick` calls `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: activeTabQueryKey })`. `isFetching` from `useIsFetching` hook or query state. Disabled while `isFetching > 0`.

    8. **`ui/src/App.tsx`**:
       - State: `const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<'inventory' | 'deployments'>('inventory')`. `const [selectedTeam, setSelectedTeam] = useState<string | null>(null)`.
       - First-load auto-select: when `useTeams()` returns data and `selectedTeam` is null, set it to the first team alphabetically (UI-SPEC Interaction Contract > Team Dropdown).
       - `useResources(selectedTeam)` provides resource data for both tabs.
       - Outer layout: `min-h-screen flex flex-col bg-background`.
       - Render `<TopNav selectedTeam={selectedTeam} onTeamChange={setSelectedTeam} activeTabQueryKey={['resources', selectedTeam]} />`.
       - Below nav: shadcn `<Tabs value={activeTab} onValueChange={(v) => setActiveTab(v as 'inventory' | 'deployments')}>` with `<TabsList>` containing Inventory and Deployments triggers, then `<TabsContent value="inventory">` → `<InventoryTable ... />` and `<TabsContent value="deployments">` → `<DeploymentsTable ... />`.
       - Content area: `flex-1 p-6 max-w-screen-xl mx-auto w-full`.
       - No-team-selected state: if `useTeams().data?.length` is 0 OR `selectedTeam` is null pre-load, show centered text per UI-SPEC States > No Team Selected.

    Mark TanStack Query mode explicitly v5 (object form). Do NOT introduce React Router. Do NOT add `refetchInterval` (D-09).
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd /Users/punitlad/projects/work/pecp-poc/ui &amp;&amp; npx tsc --noEmit &amp;&amp; npm run build</automated>
  </verify>
  <acceptance_criteria>
    - All 8 source files exist with correct paths
    - `grep -nE "useQuery<Team\\[\\]>" ui/src/hooks/useTeams.ts | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns 1 match
    - `grep -nE "useQuery<Resource\\[\\]>" ui/src/hooks/useResources.ts | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns 1 match
    - `grep -nE "queryKey: \\['resources', team\\]" ui/src/hooks/useResources.ts | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns 1 match
    - `grep -nE "fetch\\('/api/teams" ui/src/lib/api.ts | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns 1 match
    - `grep -nE "fetch\\('/api/resources" ui/src/lib/api.ts | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns at least 1 match (template literal variants accepted)
    - `grep -nE "bg-amber-100" ui/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns at least 1 match
    - `grep -nE "bg-blue-100" ui/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns at least 1 match
    - `grep -nE "bg-green-100" ui/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns at least 1 match
    - `grep -nE "bg-red-100" ui/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns at least 1 match
    - `grep -cE "<Badge variant=" ui/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx` returns 0 (UI-SPEC explicit prohibition)
    - `grep -nE "No resources found" ui/src/components/InventoryTable.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns 1 match (exact copy)
    - `grep -nE "Failed to load resources" ui/src/components/InventoryTable.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns 1 match
    - `grep -nE "PackageOpen" ui/src/components/InventoryTable.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns at least 1 match
    - `grep -nE "AlertCircle" ui/src/components/InventoryTable.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns at least 1 match
    - `grep -nE "envFilter" ui/src/components/DeploymentsTable.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns at least 1 match
    - `grep -nE "RefreshCw" ui/src/components/TopNav.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns at least 1 match
    - `grep -nE "invalidateQueries" ui/src/components/TopNav.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns at least 1 match
    - `grep -cE "refetchInterval" ui/src/` (recursive) returns 0 (D-09 invariant; no automatic polling)
    - `grep -cE "react-router" ui/package.json` returns 0 (D-07 invariant; no router)
    - `grep -nE "useState<'inventory' \\| 'deployments'>" ui/src/App.tsx | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*//'` returns 1 match
    - `cd ui && npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0
    - `cd ui && npm run build` exits 0
  </acceptance_criteria>
  <done>
    UI renders the full Inventory + Deployments contract per UI-SPEC. Status badge colors match the CLI palette. Refresh button invalidates the active query. No automatic polling. No router. Production build succeeds.
  </done>
</task>

<task type="checkpoint:human-verify" gate="blocking">
  <name>Task 3: Human verification — UI dashboard end-to-end demo walkthrough</name>
  <what-built>
    A read-only React dashboard at `ui/` with a top nav (PECP logo, team dropdown, Refresh button) and two tabs (Inventory, Deployments). Data fetched from FastAPI via Vite proxy. Status badges colored per UI-SPEC. Manual refresh only.
  </what-built>
  <how-to-verify>
    Prerequisite: Plans 01 and 02 are complete (CORS enabled, GET /teams works, seed data populated). If not, run them first.

    Setup (executor performs these steps automatically before pausing for user verification):
    1. From repo root: `python scripts/seed.py` — populates `pecp.db` with 4 teams and lifecycle-spanning resources.
    2. Start API: `uvicorn pecp.api.main:app --reload --port 8000` in one terminal.
    3. Start UI: `cd ui && npm run dev` in another terminal.
    4. Open the printed URL (default http://localhost:5173) in a browser.

    User verifies the following 12 checkpoints:

    **Top Nav:**
    1. [ ] "PECP" wordmark appears on the left in the top nav bar (h-14, border-bottom visible).
    2. [ ] Team dropdown is populated with 4 entries: `customer-product-app`, `data-platform`, `data-processing-app`, `platform-engineering` (alphabetical).
    3. [ ] Refresh button is on the right; clicking it shows the icon spinning briefly while data refetches.

    **Inventory Tab:**
    4. [ ] On initial load, Inventory tab is active (underlined with 2px primary-color border).
    5. [ ] Table renders with column headers in order: Name, Kind, Status, Environment, Project (uppercase, muted color).
    6. [ ] At least one row has a `provisioning` blue badge, one `ready` green, one `pending` amber, one `failed` red — visually matching the CLI palette.
    7. [ ] Selecting a different team in the dropdown updates the table to that team's resources.

    **Deployments Tab:**
    8. [ ] Clicking the Deployments tab shows an Environment filter dropdown above the table with options: All, dev, staging, prod.
    9. [ ] Selecting `prod` filters the table to only resources with `env=prod`. Selecting `All` restores the full list.
    10. [ ] Switching tabs does NOT trigger a new network request (verify via DevTools Network tab: no new `/api/resources` call when switching from Inventory → Deployments).

    **Refresh behavior:**
    11. [ ] Wait 30 seconds without interacting. Confirm no automatic refetch occurs in the Network tab (D-09: manual refresh only).
    12. [ ] Click Refresh. The Network tab shows a new GET to `/api/resources?team=...`. The icon spins during the fetch, then stops.

    Then verify ROADMAP success criterion #3 wording (executor updates the ROADMAP per the quality gate; see Task 4): the dashboard demonstrates "data refreshes on demand without a page reload."

    Report any visual deviation from UI-SPEC (color, spacing, copy) as a defect. The status badge palette is a hard contract.
  </how-to-verify>
  <resume-signal>Type "approved" to continue, or describe defects to be fixed.</resume-signal>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 4: Update ROADMAP.md Phase 5 success criterion #3 (D-09) and success criterion #5 (D-12)</name>
  <files>.planning/ROADMAP.md</files>
  <read_first>
    - .planning/ROADMAP.md (the Phase 5 section — confirm current SC #3 says "refreshes automatically" and current SC #5 says "2 teams, 3 projects")
    - .planning/phases/05-account-flow-ui-demo-readiness/05-CONTEXT.md D-09 (the SC #3 replacement wording) and D-12 (4 teams seed scope)
  </read_first>
  <action>
    In `.planning/ROADMAP.md`, locate the Phase 5 success criteria block and make TWO edits:

    **Edit 1 (D-09 — SC #3):** Replace success criterion #3, which currently reads:

    `3. The React dashboard loads the team resource inventory as a table with name, kind, status badge, and environment — data refreshes automatically without a page reload`

    with the D-09-mandated wording:

    `3. The React dashboard loads the team resource inventory as a table with name, kind, status badge, and environment — data refreshes on demand without a page reload`

    The change is the single phrase "refreshes automatically" → "refreshes on demand". Do not touch other wording in SC #3.

    **Edit 2 (D-12 — SC #5):** Replace success criterion #5, which currently reads:

    `5. Running the seed script populates 2 teams, 3 projects, and resources spanning all lifecycle states (`pending`, `provisioning`, `ready`, `failed`) — a stakeholder session can start from a clean database with one command`

    with the D-12-mandated wording (only the team count changes from "2 teams" to "4 teams"; project count and lifecycle list stay the same):

    `5. Running the seed script populates 4 teams, 3 projects, and resources spanning all lifecycle states (`pending`, `provisioning`, `ready`, `failed`) — a stakeholder session can start from a clean database with one command`

    The change is the single phrase "2 teams" → "4 teams". Do not touch other success criteria, the goal line, the requirements list, or the plans table for this phase.

    Both edits are mandated by locked decisions in CONTEXT.md (D-09 and D-12 respectively). Plans 05-02 and 05-04 both implement and verify the 4-team seed contract; ROADMAP SC #5 must align or it becomes a phase-close blocker.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>grep -nE "data refreshes on demand without a page reload" /Users/punitlad/projects/work/pecp-poc/.planning/ROADMAP.md &amp;&amp; grep -nE "populates 4 teams, 3 projects" /Users/punitlad/projects/work/pecp-poc/.planning/ROADMAP.md</automated>
  </verify>
  <acceptance_criteria>
    - `grep -cE "data refreshes on demand without a page reload" .planning/ROADMAP.md` returns 1
    - `grep -cE "data refreshes automatically without a page reload" .planning/ROADMAP.md` returns 0
    - `grep -cE "populates 4 teams, 3 projects" .planning/ROADMAP.md` returns 1
    - `grep -cE "populates 2 teams, 3 projects" .planning/ROADMAP.md` returns 0
    - The Phase 5 section still contains all 5 success criteria (no accidental deletions)
    - `grep -cE "### Phase 5: Account Flow \\+ UI \\+ Demo Readiness" .planning/ROADMAP.md` returns 1 (heading preserved)
  </acceptance_criteria>
  <done>
    ROADMAP.md Phase 5 SC #3 reflects the D-09 manual-refresh decision and SC #5 reflects the D-12 four-team seed decision. The UI's actual behavior (manual refresh only) and the seed script's actual scope (4 teams) now both match the success criteria.
  </done>
</task>

</tasks>

<threat_model>
## Trust Boundaries

| Boundary | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Browser → Vite dev server (5173) → FastAPI (8000) | Two-hop boundary; CORS configured server-side, proxy strips `/api` prefix client-side |
| User keyboard input (team dropdown, env filter) → React state | Bounded enum / known set; no free-text user input in this read-only UI |
| `npm` registry → ui/node_modules | Supply-chain boundary; mitigated by RESEARCH.md Package Legitimacy Audit (all packages reviewed) |

## STRIDE Threat Register

| Threat ID | Category | Component | Disposition | Mitigation Plan |
|-----------|----------|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| T-05-03-01 | Tampering | Vite proxy mis-rewrite causes 404s and silent data loss | mitigate | Acceptance criterion asserts `rewrite:.*replace.*\\^/api` is present in vite.config.ts. End-to-end checkpoint task verifies a Network tab call lands on `/resources?team=...` (no `/api` prefix at FastAPI). |
| T-05-03-02 | Tampering | Status fields rendered as raw HTML enabling XSS | mitigate | React JSX escapes by default. Status comes from `r.status` (string from API). No `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` in any component. Source assertion: `grep -c dangerouslySetInnerHTML ui/src/` returns 0 (recursive). |
| T-05-03-03 | Information disclosure | Sensitive data printed to browser console in dev | accept | TanStack devtools optional; if added, dev-only. PoC API returns no PII. |
| T-05-03-04 | Spoofing | Open CORS allowing arbitrary origins | mitigate | CORS already locked to `http://localhost:5173` in Plan 01 (D-06). UI does not need to set credentials cross-origin. |
| T-05-03-05 | Denial of service | Unbounded resource list rendered with no pagination | accept | UI-SPEC explicitly notes "no pagination for PoC — render all rows (seed data ≤ 50 rows)". Real production would virtualize. |
| T-05-03-06 | Tampering | npm postinstall scripts on supply-chain dependencies | mitigate | RESEARCH.md Package Legitimacy Audit reviewed all 7 packages; all marked SUS only due to date heuristic (false positive); none have postinstall scripts. No `[ASSUMED]` or `[SLOP]` packages remain → no human-verify checkpoint required per planner authority. |
| T-05-03-07 | Elevation of privilege | shadcn registry pulls a malicious component | mitigate | UI-SPEC Registry Safety table whitelists only the 7 official shadcn components. `npx shadcn add` only fetches from `ui.shadcn.com` registry. No third-party registries declared. |
| T-05-03-SC | Tampering | npm installs (`react`, `vite`, `tailwindcss`, `@tailwindcss/vite`, `@tanstack/react-query`, `lucide-react`, `shadcn`) | mitigate | All 7 packages explicitly reviewed in RESEARCH.md Package Legitimacy Audit; verdicts SUS-but-approved due to false-positive date heuristic; no SLOP. Standard install via official npm registry. No blocking-human checkpoint required because no `[ASSUMED]` or `[SLOP]` packages remain after audit. |
</threat_model>

2. `cd ui && npm run build` — production build succeeds.
3. End-to-end smoke (executor performs as part of Task 3 setup):
   - `python scripts/seed.py` succeeds.
   - `uvicorn pecp.api.main:app --reload` starts.
   - `cd ui && npm run dev` starts on 5173.
   - Browser loads dashboard without console errors.
4. ROADMAP success criterion #3 reflects the D-09 wording AND #5 reflects the D-12 four-team scope (Task 4 verify).
5. Human verification (Task 3) approved.
</verification>

<success_criteria>
- `ui/` builds clean with no TS or Vite errors.
- All UI-SPEC visual contracts met (status badge palette, layout structure, copy).
- Inventory and Deployments tabs both render seeded data through the live API.
- Refresh button is the only refetch trigger; no auto-polling.
- ROADMAP.md SC #3 updated per D-09 and SC #5 updated per D-12.
- Human checkpoint approves the visual + interactive demo.
</success_criteria>

- Whether `shadcn init` generated a `tailwind.config.ts` (Pitfall 4 expects no)
- Any TypeScript types divergence between `ui/src/lib/api.ts:Resource` and the actual `GET /resources` response shape (note for future contract-test work)
